---
title: "Developer Guide"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

The PRISM ecosystem consists of a backend server built with FastAPI and a web frontend built with Flutter. Both rely on Firebase for authentication and database storage. This guide provides instructions on how to set up the PRISM system locally for development or deployment.

## Firebase

The PRISM ecosystem is designed to use Firebase to securely store user data. To create your own instance of PRISM you will need a **paid Firebase project**. [Get a Firebase account and make a new project as described here](https://firebase.google.com/docs/projects/learn-more). Then follow the directions below to create your own PRISM instance.

## Unified Setup via Docker

The easiest way to install and run both the PRISM Backend and Frontend is via the [`prism-setup` repository](https://github.com/Australian-Future-Hearing-Initiative/prism-setup), which uses Docker Compose.

{{< include _setup_readme.qmd >}}


## Manual Backend Setup

If you wish to run the FastAPI backend independently without Docker, follow the instructions as detailed in the [`prism-flutter-backend` repository](https://github.com/Australian-Future-Hearing-Initiative/prism-flutter-backend).

{{< include _flutter-backend_readme.qmd >}}


## Manual Frontend Setup

If you wish to run the front end Flutter web application without docker, follow the instructions as detailed in the [`prism-flutter-web-frontend` repository](https://github.com/Australian-Future-Hearing-Initiative/prism-flutter-web-frontend).

{{< include _flutter-web-frontend_readme.qmd >}}
